title: Airline stocks take flight after Thanksgiving travel season kicks off in a big way
text: Shares of airline companies saw broad gains Wednesday, after as the latest travel data supported the expectation that Thanksgiving travel would be “busier than ever” this year. title: US airline stocks rise on expectations of record Thanksgiving travel
text: US airline stocks made gains on Wednesday as Thanksgiving travel ramps up with a record 30 million people expected to take to the skies over the holiday period. An average of 2.7 million passengers per day are expected to fly over the 12-day Thanksgiving travel period, which runs from November 17 to 28.

title: United Airlines to resume flights to Israel
text: United Airlines Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:UAL) confirmed on Monday that it is resuming flights to Tel Aviv, Israel, from Newark, New Jersey starting on November 24. The airline was one of many to suspend its flights to and from Israel's capital after the Hamas-Israel war broke out in October.

title: United Airlines tweaks frequent flyer program to reward credit card spending

title: US airlines boost winter schedules to meet surge in demand
text: U.S. carriers boosted their winter schedules in order to meet the expected surge in demand over the 2023 holiday season. Many carriers are looking at the Florida market in particular.

title: United to Offer More Flights Than Ever to Florida This Winter
text: Airline grows Florida schedule by nearly 20% compared to last winter, adding more seats and flying more planes to the most-searched Florida destinations  United also remains the nation's largest carrier to ski destinations with direct flights to nearly a dozen top winter sports hubs including Jackson Hole, Vail and Big Sky CHICAGO , Nov. 8, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- United today announced it will offer more flights to Florida this winter than it has in the airline's history – growing its schedule by nearly 20% compared to last winter with more flights to popular sun destinations including Miami, Tampa and Orlando and by flying larger planes with more seats. For the first time in the company's history, United will fly mainline aircraft and nearly double the amount of seats between Key West International Airport and several United Hub airports including Chicago O'Hare, Washington Dulles, Houston, and Newark/New York.

title: Dow transports have best week in a year, to snap longest weekly losing streak in 5 years
text: The Dow Jones Transportation Average DJT, +1.78% rallied 1.7% in afternoon trading, with 18 of 20 components gaining ground, as part of a broader stock market rally. The index was headed for a fifth-straight gain as it has run up 7.2% this week.
